# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].
# Category: sql_injection

class ReportsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_admin_user! # Assuming you have an admin user authentication mechanism

  def index
    # Renders the form to input the SQL query
  end

  def generate
    sql_query = params[:query]

    if sql_query.present?
      begin
        # Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute to run the raw SQL query
        @results = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute(sql_query)
        # @results will be an array of hashes for SELECT queries
        # or a single integer for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE queries (number of affected rows)
      rescue ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid => e
        @error = "SQL Error: #{e.message}"
      rescue => e
        @error = "An unexpected error occurred: #{e.message}"
      end
    else
      @error = "SQL query cannot be empty."
    end

    render :index # Renders the index view to display results or errors
  end
end
